findings.title: Convex extra rewards are claimed to the pool but sold from the RewardManager, permanently stranding them

best_agent_match.title: Convex extra rewards are claimed to the pool but sold from the RewardManager, permanently stranding them

findings.title: The first staker after a zero-staker period can capture all rewards accrued while nobody was staked

best_agent_match.title: All rewards accrued during zero-staker periods can be stolen by the next staker

findings.title: RewardManager pre-books CVX with single-cliff math that can exceed what Convex will actually mint

best_agent_match.title: RewardManager books estimated CVX before claim using cliff math that can overstate actual minting

findings.title: Permissionless depeg handling can offboard healthy pools because it relies on a stale price cache

best_agent_match.title: Permissionless depeg handling relies on a stale price cache that is only refreshed on weight updates

findings.title: Small deposits and withdrawals can revert because allocation rounding leaves no selectable pool

best_agent_match.title: Small deposits and withdrawals can revert because target-allocation rounding leaves no selectable pool

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on handleInvalidConvexPid
rejected_candidates.reason: Permissionless access is intentional here and still gated by `isShutdownPid(pid)`, so this is not an authorization bug by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Slippage Protection Bypass via Unsupported Tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: Under the current reward-claim path, Convex extra rewards are sent to the pool rather than the RewardManager, so the reported unsupported-token swap path is not reachable in normal protocol operation.

rejected_candidates.title: No Deadline on DEX Swaps
rejected_candidates.reason: The Sushi swap already uses `block.timestamp` as a deadline, and the Curve `exchange` interface used here does not expose a separate deadline parameter.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Reentrancy via functionDelegateCall
rejected_candidates.reason: This depends on a malicious or compromised handler contract configured by trusted governance/controller components; no same-trust-boundary reentrancy path was substantiated.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Curve Pool Exchange Return Values
rejected_candidates.reason: Curve `exchange` returns an amount and reverts on failure; the code is not ignoring a boolean success flag.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by Zero in ScaledMath.divDown
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic library property, not a concrete exploitable protocol issue from the reviewed call sites.

rejected_candidates.title: Reward Calculation Vulnerable to MEV/Front-Running
rejected_candidates.reason: `claimableRewards` is a view function and the report did not establish a permissionless way to reduce pool-held rewards before claims.

rejected_candidates.title: Lack of Event Emitting for Critical State Changes
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing events are an observability issue, not a protocol-impacting vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Centralization Risk - Owner Has Excessive Privileges
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a governance/trust-model concern rather than a code vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Insufficient Validation in addCurvePool
rejected_candidates.reason: This is again an owner-governance quality concern; it does not show a permissionless or unintended exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero Address Check in setExtraRewardsCurvePool
rejected_candidates.reason: `address(0)` is explicitly supported and means 'do not use a Curve pool', falling back to the Sushi path.

rejected_candidates.title: Infinite Approval for External Contracts
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a standard trust assumption toward protocol-controlled components, not a standalone vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Lack of Input Validation in updateWeights
rejected_candidates.reason: The reported issue is a policy preference about extreme controller-set weights, not a concrete bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Integer Overflow in ScaledMath.mulDown
rejected_candidates.reason: Solidity 0.8.x already reverts on overflow; no practical overflow exploit was shown.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Sanity Checks in withdraw
rejected_candidates.reason: Withdrawals are bounded by actual balances and protected by `minUnderlyingReceived`; the report did not demonstrate a silent loss beyond user-accepted slippage.

rejected_candidates.title: Anyone Can Trigger poolCheckpoint
rejected_candidates.reason: `poolCheckpoint()` only updates accounting state and does not transfer rewards to the caller; no theft path was substantiated.

rejected_candidates.title: Lack of Access Control on setFeePercentage
rejected_candidates.reason: The function is `onlyOwner` and capped; this is a governance-policy complaint, not missing access control.

rejected_candidates.title: Unprotected receive() Function Allows ETH Stuck
rejected_candidates.reason: For non-WETH pools the `receive()` function reverts, so ETH is not silently trapped through the path described.
